candidates.comparison: async TP with fused gemm comms compile pass vs standard TP (unfused baseline)
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: agree — compare_two_settings performs exact dict equality on generated text, token IDs, finish reasons, and usage stats; this is a weak oracle
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: agree — async TP fused gemm comms compile pass changes kernel selection and communication/computation overlap, altering FP accumulation order which can change greedy-decoded text
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: agree — Not Strong #5: async TP / fused distributed compile-pass parity against unfused baseline; Not Strong #2: compiled (VLLM_COMPILE with fuse_gemm_comms) vs non-compiled baseline in compare_two_settings
candidates.code_snippet:
compare_two_settings(
    model_id,
    async_tp_args,
    tp_args,
    method="generate",
    force_v1_runner=True,
)

candidates.comparison: async TP with SP + fused gemm comms compile pass vs standard TP (NVFP4 model)
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: agree — compare_two_settings performs exact dict equality; weak oracle
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: agree — compile pass enables SP and fused gemm comms changing kernel selection, communication patterns, and accumulation order; NVFP4 quantization adds further FP sensitivity
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: agree — Not Strong #3: different compile strategies / fused distributed compile passes versus baseline; Not Strong #5: async TP / fused distributed compile-pass parity against unfused baseline
candidates.code_snippet:
compare_two_settings(
    NVFP4_MODEL_ID,
    async_tp_args,
    tp_args,
    method="generate",
    force_v1_runner=True,
)

candidates.comparison: sequence parallel compile pass (with various PP/TP/eager/chunked-prefill combos) vs standard TP
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: agree — compare_two_settings performs exact dict equality on generated output dicts; weak oracle
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: agree — SP partitions computation differently across TP ranks changing GEMM shapes and accumulation order; the compile pass changes kernel selection; both produce different FP rounding
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: agree — Not Strong #5: sequence parallel compile-pass parity against unfused TP baseline; Not Strong #3: tp_sp_args uses VLLM_COMPILE while tp_args uses default/eager
candidates.code_snippet:
compare_two_settings(
    model_id,
    tp_sp_args,
    tp_args,
    method="generate",
    force_v1_runner=True,
)

candidates.comparison: sequence parallel compile pass with prompt_embeds enabled vs standard TP (both sides use prompt_embeds)
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: agree — compare_two_settings performs exact dict equality; weak oracle
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: agree — SP changes computation partitioning and kernel selection producing different FP rounding that can change greedy-decoded text
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: agree — Not Strong #5: sequence parallel compile-pass parity against unfused TP baseline; Not Strong #3: different compile strategies versus baseline. Phase 1 also cited Not Strong #9 (prompt_embeds vs standard prompt) but this is imprecise since both sides use prompt_embeds; however #5 and #3 are sufficient

candidates.comparison: sequence parallel compile pass with NVFP4 quantization (fuse_norm_quant + fuse_act_quant) vs standard TP; eager_mode=True but SP side still uses VLLM_COMPILE
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: agree — compare_two_settings performs exact dict equality; weak oracle
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: agree — SP with fused norm/act quantization changes kernel selection and computation partitioning; NVFP4 quantization kernels are sensitive to FP accumulation order
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: agree — Not Strong #5: sequence parallel compile-pass parity against unfused TP baseline; Not Strong #3: tp_sp_args uses VLLM_COMPILE with fused passes while tp_args has no compile config
candidates.code_snippet:
_compare_sp(
    NVFP4_MODEL_ID,
    ParallelSetup(tp_size=2, pp_size=1, fuse_norm_quant=True, fuse_act_quant=True, eager_mode=True, chunked_prefill=False),
    "mp", "auto",
    ...
    method="generate",
    is_multimodal=False,
    dtype="bfloat16",
)
